name: Chang'e
shortDescription: Chang'e is the Chinese moon goddess, known for consuming an elixir of immortality and ascending to the moon. She is a central figure in the Mid-Autumn Festival celebrations.
description: Chang'e is a prominent figure in Chinese mythology, most famously known as the moon goddess. Her story varies across different versions, but the core narrative involves her husband, Houyi (also known as Yi the Archer), a skilled archer who shot down nine of the ten suns that were scorching the Earth. As a reward for his heroism, the Queen Mother of the West, Xi Wangmu, gifted him an elixir of immortality. In some versions, Houyi intended to share the elixir with Chang'e, but in others, she stole it. Driven by curiosity, ambition, or perhaps to prevent a villain from acquiring it, Chang'e consumed the elixir. Upon doing so, she became weightless and ascended to the moon. Some versions claim she was transformed into a toad as punishment, while others depict her as a beautiful, solitary figure residing in the moon palace, attended by a jade rabbit. She is often associated with the Mid-Autumn Festival, a celebration where families gather to admire the full moon and eat mooncakes in her honor. Her story explores themes of immortality, sacrifice, and the consequences of one's actions. Chang'e's legend continues to be a source of inspiration in Chinese culture, representing beauty, longing, and the mysteries of the cosmos.
quests: Accidental/intentional consumption of the elixir of immortality

sources.title: In Search of the Woman Warrior: Four Myth-Figures of Ancient China
sources.author: Yenna Wu
